text:
Artificial intelligence is changing how we think about creativity. Today, AI can generate paintings, compose music, and even write articles or poems. For example, you can ask an AI to create an image in the style of a famous artist, and it will produce something new in seconds. Similarly, AI systems can write short stories or songs that sound surprisingly human.

However, this raises an important question: is AI truly creative? Human creativity comes from personal experiences, emotions, and imagination. Machines, on the other hand, learn from large amounts of existing data and follow patterns. They can produce impressive results, but they do not feel or intend meaning. So, while AI can be a useful tool for inspiration, it cannot replace the unique creativity that makes us human.
